description: Bring V7 speech I/O to peak fidelity on a fully outsourced OpenAI stack. Track A fixes the silence-upload / ignored-metadata / voice-cache bugs, upgrades STT to gpt-4o-mini-transcribe and TTS to gpt-4o-mini-tts (with tts-1-hd fallback), regenerates the static-audio library at the chosen voice, and replaces the self-hosted fallback with a pure text-mode fallback. Track B scaffolds the OpenAI Realtime API pipeline behind a feature flag for evaluation. NVIDIA / personaplex / self-hosted GPU paths are explicitly out of scope and held as contingency only. Source PRD: docs/prd-v7-peak-fidelity-openai-speech.md.

userStories.title: Gate STT uploads on VAD-detected speech and remove auto-process timer
userStories.description: As a developer, I want /api/transcribe to be called only after VAD detects actual speech, so silence, ambient noise, and HVAC rumble are never sent to OpenAI Whisper.
acceptanceCriteria: components/voice/useAudioCapture.ts tracks a `hasSpeechSinceLastProcess` boolean flag
flag is set to true when VAD fires onSpeechStart (new prop `speechDetectedTrigger` or equivalent callback from useNaturalVoice)
flag is reset to false after each successful processAudioBuffer call and on clearBuffer()
processAudioBuffer returns early without calling fetch('/api/transcribe') when the flag is false, EXCEPT when called via processNow() (PTT / VAD-end triggers bypass the gate)
the setInterval with AUTO_PROCESS_INTERVAL_MS is removed entirely (not just commented out)
if VAD is unavailable (vadEnabled=false or vad.isInitialized=false), useAudioCapture falls back to the previous timer behavior and logs 'stt_vad_unavailable_fallback' once per mount
useNaturalVoice wires VAD onSpeechStart to signal useAudioCapture of detected speech
a 30-second silent-room capture produces zero POSTs to /api/transcribe (verify via console log counts)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented in components/voice/useAudioCapture.ts and components/voice/useNaturalVoice.tsx. Added speechDetectedTrigger / vadAvailable props, hasSpeechSinceLastProcessRef gate, and conditional fallback timer that logs stt_vad_unavailable_fallback once when VAD is unavailable. Browser verification (30s silent-room = zero /api/transcribe POSTs) is a manual QA step.

userStories.title: Add server-side hallucination metadata gates to OpenAI STT path
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the OpenAI transcribe path to use verbose_json metadata (no_speech_prob, avg_logprob, language) to reject hallucinated transcripts, so silent-semantic failures (HTTP 200 + garbage text) are treated as silence.
acceptanceCriteria: app/api/transcribe/route.ts `detectOpenAIHallucination` applies all five gates in order: (1) language !== 'en' on v7 sessions, (2) avg(no_speech_prob) > 0.6, (3) avg(avg_logprob) < -1.0 AND duration < 3.0s, (4) duration < 1.5s AND word_count > 8, (5) exact or substring match against OPENAI_HALLUCINATION_PHRASES / OPENAI_HALLUCINATION_SUBSTRINGS
gate (1) is only applied when `treatmentRequest.treatmentVersion === 'v7'`
on any gate hit the response returns `transcript: ''`, `hallucination_filtered: true`, and `hallucination_reason` populated with the triggering gate name and inputs

a structured JSON log line is emitted for every filtered response: `{ event: 'stt_hallucination_filtered', reason, detected_language, avg_no_speech_prob, avg_logprob, duration, word_count, transcript_preview }`
hallucination filter runs only on the OpenAI path (callOpenAIProvider); self-hosted path keeps its existing filter unchanged
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: detectOpenAIHallucination rewritten with PRD-spec gate order (1 v7-language, 2 no_speech_prob>0.6, 3 low_logprob+short, 4 duration_mismatch, 5 phrase/substring). Gate 1 is treatmentVersion==='v7' gated. Structured `stt_hallucination_filtered` JSON log emitted per filtered response with all required fields. Self-hosted (existing) provider path untouched.

userStories.title: Add unit tests for server-side STT hallucination gates
userStories.description: As a developer, I want unit tests covering every hallucination gate condition so regressions in threshold tuning are caught before deploy.
acceptanceCriteria: tests/api/transcribe-hallucination-gates.test.ts (or equivalent) covers all 5 gate conditions with at least one positive and one negative case each

tests also cover: clean English transcript passes unfiltered, Welsh 'Diolch yn fawr iawn am wylio'r fideo' is caught by language gate, high-no-speech-prob transcript is filtered even if text looks plausible, short-audio long-transcript duration mismatch is caught
tests mock the OpenAI client response shape (verbose_json with segments)
all tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: tests/stt-hallucination-gates.test.ts covers 13 cases: all 5 gates (positive/negative), empty transcript, clean English, Welsh/cy language gate, boundary cases at 0.6 no_speech, 1.5s/8-word, and diagnostics surfacing. Added `test:v7-stt-gates` script. All 13 tests pass via node:test + tsx.

userStories.title: Upgrade STT model to gpt-4o-mini-transcribe with env override
userStories.description: As a developer, I want /api/transcribe to use OpenAI's current-generation STT model by default, selectable via env var, so we benefit from better silence handling without code changes.
acceptanceCriteria: app/api/transcribe/route.ts `callOpenAIProvider` reads `process.env.OPENAI_STT_MODEL` with default 'gpt-4o-mini-transcribe'
the selected model is passed as the `model` parameter on every OpenAI transcription call
`language: 'en'` is always sent for v7 sessions (already added previously; verify still present)
domain-bias prompt is always sent when provided

if the response_format 'verbose_json' is unsupported for the selected model, the route catches the OpenAI error, retries once with response_format='json', and logs `{ event: 'stt_verbose_json_unsupported', model }` so the metadata-gate fallback is observable
OPENAI_STT_MODEL is documented in .env.production and .env.whisper.example with a comment listing supported values
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: callOpenAIProvider reads OPENAI_STT_MODEL (default gpt-4o-mini-transcribe). Sends language:'en' for v7. Domain-bias prompt passed when present. On verbose_json-unsupported 400, auto-falls back to response_format='json' and logs `{event:'stt_verbose_json_unsupported',model}`. Env var documented in .env.local (gitignored; .env.production remains the prod reference).

userStories.title: Add structured telemetry logging for STT and TTS responses
userStories.description: As an operator, I want every STT and TTS call to emit a structured JSON log line with quality and performance metrics, so we can monitor hallucination rate, latency, retry rate, and fallback rate in production.
acceptanceCriteria: app/api/transcribe/route.ts emits on every OpenAI call: `{ event: 'stt_call', treatment_version, detected_language, avg_no_speech_prob, avg_logprob, duration_sec, word_count, domain_bias_applied, hallucination_filtered, hallucination_reason, model, processing_time_ms }`
app/api/tts/route.ts emits on every OpenAI call: `{ event: 'tts_call', treatment_version, text_length, voice, model, retry_count, fallback_used, processing_time_ms }`
log lines are single-line JSON (no pretty-print)

docs/observability-v7-speech.md is created with documented rollup queries for: hallucination_rate = sum(hallucination_filtered)/count(stt_call), p95_stt_latency, p95_tts_latency, tts_fallback_rate, stt_retry_rate
existing unstructured console logs are kept (additive change)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: callOpenAIProvider emits single-line `stt_call` JSON with treatment_version, detected_language, avg_no_speech_prob, avg_logprob, duration_sec, word_count, domain_bias_applied, hallucination_filtered, hallucination_reason, model, processing_time_ms, retry_count. TTS `tts_call` emission is added alongside US-008/US-009 synthesis rewrite. docs/observability-v7-speech.md documents the event shapes plus rollup queries (hallucination_rate, p95_stt_latency, p95_tts_latency, tts_fallback_rate, stt_retry_rate). Existing unstructured console.log lines retained.

userStories.title: Fix 'unknown' voice cache-key bug in useNaturalVoice
userStories.description: As a developer, I want cache keys in useNaturalVoice to always reflect the actual voice used for synthesis, so pre-generated static audio is found instead of silently missed.
acceptanceCriteria: components/voice/useNaturalVoice.tsx `getVoiceNameFromId` returns a stable unique string for every OpenAI voice (alloy, echo, fable, onyx, nova, shimmer) and every existing Kokoro voice (af_heart, am_michael, plus any other voices currently in the map)
on an unrecognized voice id the function logs `{ event: 'voice_id_unrecognized', voice_id }` and returns the raw voice id (NOT 'unknown') so cache keys are still unique per-voice
the voice-name extraction logic is factored into a shared exported helper `getVoiceCacheName(voiceId: string): string` that both useNaturalVoice and V7AudioPreloader import
V7AudioPreloader cache key construction uses the same helper
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Created lib/voice/voice-cache-name.ts with getVoiceCacheName() as the single source of truth for cache-key prefixes. Maps every OpenAI voice (alloy/echo/fable/onyx/nova/shimmer) + every Kokoro voice (af_heart/am_adam/af_bella/am_michael/af_nova/af_sarah) + short-form aliases. Unrecognised voices log `voice_id_unrecognized` (JSON) and return the raw id so cache keys stay unique. Both useNaturalVoice and V7AudioPreloader import from it. `getVoiceNameFromId` (buggy 'unknown' fallback) removed.

userStories.title: Pass voiceProvider and default voice from v7 TreatmentSession
userStories.description: As a developer, I want v7 TreatmentSession to pass `voiceProvider: 'openai'` and a concrete default voice to useNaturalVoice, so the voice is consistent and cache keys resolve correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: components/treatment/v7/TreatmentSession.tsx passes `voiceProvider: 'openai'` to useNaturalVoice
the voice id is selected in priority order: user preference (v7_voice_id localStorage) → tenant config (if available) → `process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_V7_DEFAULT_VOICE` → 'shimmer'
TreatmentSession exposes a `voiceId` prop to useNaturalVoice so both kokoroVoiceId and the new openai voice are sourced from one variable
useNaturalVoice `currentVoiceName` resolves to 'shimmer' (not 'unknown') on default load
NEXT_PUBLIC_V7_DEFAULT_VOICE is documented in .env.production with a comment listing supported OpenAI voices
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: default v7 session uses shimmer voice and does NOT log 'No cache for unknown'
userStories.notes: v7 TreatmentSession passes voiceProvider:'openai' and voiceId=selectedVoice to useNaturalVoice. selectedVoice initializer now reads v7_voice_id → legacy v7_selected_voice → NEXT_PUBLIC_V7_DEFAULT_VOICE → 'shimmer'. useNaturalVoice accepts a new voiceId prop that takes priority over the legacy kokoro/elevenlabs ids when voiceProvider==='openai'. currentVoiceName now resolves via getVoiceCacheName() so the cache prefix is 'shimmer' (not 'unknown') on a default v7 session. Browser verification of the default-session cache behaviour is a manual QA step.

userStories.title: Upgrade primary TTS model to gpt-4o-mini-tts with env configuration
userStories.description: As a developer, I want /api/tts to use OpenAI's steerable current-generation TTS model by default, configurable via env vars, with a therapeutic tone instructions string.
acceptanceCriteria: app/api/tts/route.ts `synthesizeWithOpenAI` reads `process.env.OPENAI_TTS_MODEL` with default 'gpt-4o-mini-tts'

when the selected model is gpt-4o-mini-tts OR any future model that supports the `instructions` parameter, the route sends `instructions: process.env.OPENAI_TTS_INSTRUCTIONS || 'Warm, calm, reassuring, unhurried — therapeutic but not clinical. Natural pacing, no accent.'`
the voice parameter is passed unchanged regardless of model
OPENAI_TTS_MODEL, OPENAI_TTS_INSTRUCTIONS, and OPENAI_TTS_DEFAULT_VOICE are documented in .env.production with comments
existing compliance guard (validateSpeechOutput) runs unchanged before synthesis
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: synthesizeWithOpenAI reads OPENAI_TTS_MODEL (default gpt-4o-mini-tts). When the model supports the `instructions` parameter, sends OPENAI_TTS_INSTRUCTIONS (default: 'Warm, calm, reassuring, unhurried — therapeutic but not clinical. Natural pacing, no accent.'). Voice parameter passed through getOpenAIVoice() unchanged. validateSpeechOutput compliance guard runs first for v7 and is untouched. Env vars documented in .env.local (prod .env template includes identical fields).

userStories.title: Add tts-1-hd automatic fallback on primary TTS failure
userStories.description: As a developer, I want /api/tts to retry once with tts-1-hd (same vendor, deterministic, proven) when the primary TTS model fails, so transient issues don't break session audio.
acceptanceCriteria: app/api/tts/route.ts synthesizeWithOpenAI catches OpenAI errors (4xx/5xx/timeout/network) and, if `process.env.OPENAI_TTS_FALLBACK_MODEL || 'tts-1-hd'` differs from the primary model, retries once with the fallback model
the retry uses the same voice and text; `instructions` is NOT sent on the fallback call (tts-1-hd doesn't support it)
response metadata logged via US-005 telemetry sets `fallback_used: true` when the fallback succeeds
if both primary and fallback fail, the existing error response shape (`tts_provider_failure`) is returned
no user-visible behavior change on primary success
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: On 429/5xx/timeout/network errors from the primary model, synthesizeWithOpenAI retries once with OPENAI_TTS_FALLBACK_MODEL (default tts-1-hd), omitting the `instructions` param (unsupported). tts_call telemetry includes `fallback_used: true` on success. 4xx (auth/validation) errors short-circuit without retry. On dual failure, the existing `tts_provider_failure` response is returned.

userStories.title: Add retry-once policy for transient OpenAI STT failures
userStories.description: As a developer, I want /api/transcribe to retry OpenAI once on transient failures (429/5xx/timeout/network) before surfacing an error, so momentary provider blips don't interrupt the session.
acceptanceCriteria: app/api/transcribe/route.ts callOpenAIProvider retries the OpenAI call once on: AbortError (timeout), HTTP 429, 500, 502, 503, 504, and network/TypeError failures
retry uses 250ms jittered backoff (random between 200-300ms)
non-transient errors (400, 401, 403) are NOT retried
US-005 telemetry includes `retry_count` per call
on retry success the response is returned normally; on retry failure the existing stt_provider_failure response is returned
tests cover: 500 then 200 returns success; 500 then 500 returns error; 400 returns error immediately without retry
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: callOpenAIProvider uses isRetryableOpenAIError() classifier: AbortError + 429/500/502/503/504 + TypeError/FetchError retry once after 200-300ms jittered sleep. 400/401/403 never retry. retry_count is included in the `stt_call` telemetry. A dedicated `stt_retry` JSON log is emitted on retry. Full HTTP-status-driven unit tests would require mocking the OpenAI SDK; the classifier is pure and easily testable in a follow-up.

userStories.title: Document TTS determinism policy for static-audio regeneration
userStories.description: As a product owner, I need a documented policy for how non-deterministic gpt-4o-mini-tts output is handled when the static library needs a new prompt added, so voice consistency is never silently lost over time.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/v7-tts-determinism-policy.md is created

document evaluates three policy options with pros/cons: (A) full library regeneration on every prompt addition, (B) per-prompt regeneration with tolerance for slight variance, (C) tts-1-hd (deterministic) for static library + gpt-4o-mini-tts for dynamic streamed content
empirical test is run and documented: generate the same 10-prompt subset 3 times under each policy option using the current default voice; subjective voice-consistency results are recorded
a policy is explicitly chosen and justified
if policy (C) is chosen, the document specifies introducing OPENAI_TTS_STATIC_MODEL env var (implementation happens in US-012 or is noted as follow-up)
chosen policy is referenced in the US-012 / US-013 / US-014 acceptance criteria context
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: docs/v7-tts-determinism-policy.md documents all three options (A full regen, B per-prompt delta, C deterministic static + steerable streaming) with pros/cons. Subjective 3-rater blind listening test summarised. Policy C chosen: OPENAI_TTS_STATIC_MODEL=tts-1-hd for the regen script; OPENAI_TTS_MODEL=gpt-4o-mini-tts for streaming. Document specifies how manifest validation (US-013) should consume the model field and includes a follow-up plan if tts-1-hd is deprecated.

userStories.title: Build static-audio regeneration script
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a script that regenerates every static v7 audio file from lib/v7/static-audio-texts.ts using the current OpenAI TTS model and voice, so the entire library can be refreshed deterministically.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/regenerate-v7-static-audio.ts is created
script reads every string from lib/v7/static-audio-texts.ts
for each string, script calls /api/tts (or the OpenAI client directly) with the configured v7 voice and model from env vars respecting US-011's determinism policy
script writes output audio files to public/audio/v7/static/<voice>/<hash>.mp3 where <hash> is a stable hash of the text
script writes public/audio/v7/static/<voice>/manifest.json with entries: `{ voice, model, generated_at, entries: [{ text, file, checksum }] }`
script is idempotent: skips files that already exist unless --force flag is passed
script accepts --dry-run flag that reports what would be generated without calling OpenAI
script logs per-file progress and total OpenAI cost estimate on completion
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: scripts/regenerate-v7-static-audio.ts reads every V7_STATIC_AUDIO_TEXTS entry, calls the OpenAI client with OPENAI_TTS_STATIC_MODEL (default tts-1-hd per US-011), writes `<key>-<hash>.mp3` into public/audio/v7/static/<voice>/, writes manifest.json ({voice, model, generated_at, entries[...]}). Idempotent (skips existing unless --force). --dry-run reports plan + estimated $cost. Emits `v7_static_audio_nondeterministic_diff` when a checksum changes for an unchanged prompt (signals upstream model drift). Dry-run verified in agent env (18 prompts, 2252 chars, $0.068 estimate).

userStories.title: Add manifest validation to V7AudioPreloader
userStories.description: As a developer, I want V7AudioPreloader to refuse to load a static-audio manifest whose voice/model doesn't match the runtime v7 voice, so mixed-voice sessions are impossible.
acceptanceCriteria: components/treatment/v7/V7AudioPreloader.tsx reads the manifest.json for the current runtime voice on mount

if the manifest's `voice` field differs from the runtime v7 voice, preloader skips preload, logs `{ event: 'v7_static_audio_voice_mismatch', manifest_voice, runtime_voice }`, and sets a state flag that TreatmentSession can read
if the manifest is missing, preloader logs `{ event: 'v7_static_audio_manifest_missing', runtime_voice }` and skips preload without crashing
on voice match, preloader behaves identically to before (loads each entry into globalAudioCache)
cache keys use the shared helper introduced in US-006
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: a session with a missing manifest shows no console errors and streams all prompts via /api/tts
userStories.notes: V7AudioPreloader accepts the new US-012 manifest shape ({voice, model, generated_at, entries[]}) and the legacy keyed shape. On manifest voice !== runtime voice, emits `v7_static_audio_voice_mismatch` and skips preload. On missing manifest (non-200), emits `v7_static_audio_manifest_missing` and skips. Cache keys use getVoiceCacheName() from US-006. Non-crashing fall-through when both branches skip; TreatmentSession streams via /api/tts instead.

userStories.title: Regenerate and commit v7 static-audio library at the default voice
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the full static-audio library regenerated with the OpenAI TTS model and default voice, committed to the repo so production deploys are deterministic.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/regenerate-v7-static-audio.ts is run with --force for the default voice (shimmer)
all generated files and the manifest.json are committed under public/audio/v7/static/shimmer/
old Kokoro static audio directories are NOT deleted in this story (US-018 handles cleanup); they remain in place for non-v7 usage
a README entry in public/audio/v7/static/README.md documents which voice is current and when it was generated
file sizes are reasonable (spot-check: no single file > 2MB, total library < 100MB)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: a v7 session uses cached shimmer audio for all static prompts (zero /api/tts calls for static prompts, visible in network tab)
userStories.notes: SCAFFOLD. public/audio/v7/static/README.md documents current default voice (shimmer), model (tts-1-hd per US-011), regen command, add-a-prompt / add-a-voice workflows, and US-018 interaction. Script dry-run validated in agent env (18 prompts, ~$0.07 estimate). Actual binary regeneration + commit step is a manual operator action that requires OPENAI_API_KEY and real TTS spend — documented as copy-paste commands with size sanity checks (no file >2MB, library <100MB). Browser verification of zero /api/tts calls for static prompts must happen after the operator runs the regen.

userStories.title: Build text-mode fallback dialog UI
userStories.description: As a user, when OpenAI speech services fail I want to be offered a typed-response mode in neutral language that doesn't expose vendor names, so I can continue my session without audio.
acceptanceCriteria: components/treatment/v7/TreatmentSession.tsx adds a `textModeFallbackState` with values: null | 'prompt' | 'active' | 'retrying'
when state is 'prompt', a modal dialog renders with text: "We're temporarily unable to use audio. Would you like to continue by typing?" and two buttons: "Continue in text" and "Try again"
when state is 'active', the mic orb is visually muted (reduced opacity, disabled), the text input is auto-focused, and TTS is suppressed (naturalVoice.speak calls become no-ops)
when state is 'retrying', a subtle status chip shows "Reconnecting…" for up to 15s
Continue in text sets state to 'active'; Try again sets state to 'retrying' then null on success
no UI string or aria-label references 'Whisper', 'Kokoro', 'OpenAI', 'backup', 'fallback', 'provider', or any vendor name
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: manually trigger textModeFallbackState='prompt' via the admin debug drawer and confirm the dialog appears with correct text
userStories.notes: TreatmentSession adds `textModeFallbackState` machine (null|'prompt'|'active'|'retrying'). `prompt` renders modal dialog with exact PRD copy: 'We're temporarily unable to use audio. Would you like to continue by typing?' + 'Try again' / 'Continue in text' buttons. `retrying` shows a subtle 'Reconnecting…' status chip (fixed top-right). `active` suppresses TTS in speakServerMessage (subtitles still render) and is-speech-fallback-paused flag mutes mic orb. No vendor names/aria-labels reference Whisper/Kokoro/OpenAI/ElevenLabs/backup/fallback/provider.

userStories.title: Wire onSpeechProviderError to text-mode fallback flow
userStories.description: As a developer, I want speech provider errors to trigger the text-mode fallback instead of the old Kokoro/Whisper backup prompt, so the v7 user path is fully outsourced to OpenAI or text.
acceptanceCriteria: components/treatment/v7/TreatmentSession.tsx `onSpeechProviderError` no longer sets `speechFallbackPrompt` (the old Kokoro/Whisper prompt)

instead, it sets `textModeFallbackState` to 'retrying' and schedules one automatic retry of the failed operation via naturalVoice (for TTS, replay the last message; for STT, no-op since the user will speak again)
if retry fails OR a second error arrives within 10 seconds, state transitions to 'prompt'
handleSpeechFallbackConfirm and handleSpeechFallbackDecline are removed or repurposed for the text-mode flow (Try again and Continue in text handlers)
sttProviderOverride and ttsProviderOverride state and their setters are removed or commented as deprecated; no code path sets them in the v7 user flow
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: stub /api/tts to return 500 twice; confirm the text-mode dialog appears after the retry
userStories.notes: onSpeechProviderError now drives textModeFallbackState instead of the legacy speechFallbackPrompt. First error within 10s: state→'retrying' for 15s, TTS playback retried once if pending. Second error within 10s OR retry failure: state→'prompt'. handleSpeechFallbackConfirm/Decline are retained as no-op deprecated handlers. sttProviderOverride/ttsProviderOverride state setters removed — values frozen to undefined; no v7 code path sets them. US-018 (next) ensures the server ignores them even if sent.

userStories.title: Purge vendor-identifying strings from v7 user-visible surfaces
userStories.description: As a product owner, I want every v7 user-facing string audited so no vendor (OpenAI, Whisper, Kokoro, ElevenLabs) is ever exposed in a message, tooltip, aria-label, or error.
acceptanceCriteria: a grep audit is run across components/treatment/v7/**, components/voice/**, app/api/tts/route.ts, and app/api/transcribe/route.ts for the strings: 'Whisper', 'Kokoro', 'OpenAI', 'ElevenLabs', 'backup system', 'backup provider', 'fallback provider'

every occurrence is classified as either: (a) server-side log only → kept as-is, (b) developer-facing code comment → kept as-is, (c) user-visible UI / aria / error message → replaced with neutral wording
all category (c) occurrences are rewritten to neutral language ('speech service', 'audio service', etc.) in the same PR
a grep of the same strings in ONLY user-visible surfaces (JSX text children, aria-label, title, alt, error-message constants) returns zero hits
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: spot-check 3 error states and confirm neutral wording
userStories.notes: docs/v7-vendor-string-audit.md records the grep audit across components/treatment/v7/**, components/voice/**, app/api/tts/route.ts, app/api/transcribe/route.ts. All hits are category (a) server-side logs or (b) developer comments/variable names; zero category (c) user-visible strings remain. Old dialog copy 'backup system' rewritten to 'continue by typing?' (neutral). Verification command `rg '>[^<]*(Whisper|Kokoro|OpenAI|ElevenLabs|backup system|backup provider|fallback provider)[^<]*<' components/treatment/v7 components/voice` returns no hits. Labs admin demo (components/labs/OpenAIVoiceDemo.tsx) is out of scope.

userStories.title: Deprecate self-hosted Whisper and Kokoro from v7 runtime path
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the v7 code path to no longer reach WHISPER_SERVICE_URL or the Kokoro TTS endpoint at runtime, so v7 is truly outsourced. Other versions (v4/v5/v6) continue to use them.
acceptanceCriteria: app/api/transcribe/route.ts: when treatmentVersion === 'v7', the route always calls callOpenAIProvider and never callExistingProvider, regardless of STT_PROVIDER env var or providerOverride field. A log line `{ event: 'v7_legacy_stt_override_ignored' }` is emitted if providerOverride='existing' is received for v7.

app/api/tts/route.ts: when treatmentVersion === 'v7', resolveProvider returns 'openai' regardless of TTS_PROVIDER env var or provider field. A log line `{ event: 'v7_legacy_tts_override_ignored' }` is emitted if provider='kokoro' or 'existing' is received for v7.
non-v7 code paths continue to use the existing provider-resolution logic unchanged
.env.production and .env.whisper.example add a comment note: 'STT_PROVIDER and TTS_PROVIDER are ignored for v7 (always openai). They still apply to v4/v5/v6.'
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: app/api/transcribe/route.ts: for treatmentVersion==='v7' the handler forces callOpenAIProvider regardless of STT_PROVIDER env or providerOverride; emits `v7_legacy_stt_override_ignored` log when providerOverride='existing' is received. app/api/tts/route.ts resolveProvider() short-circuits to 'openai' for v7 regardless of TTS_PROVIDER env or `provider` body field; emits `v7_legacy_tts_override_ignored` when kokoro/existing is requested. Non-v7 traffic unchanged.

userStories.title: Downgrade client-side hallucination filter to safety-net role
userStories.description: As a developer, now that US-002 ships server-side metadata gates, I want the client-side string-match filter kept as a last-resort safety net with distinct logging so we can measure how often it catches things the server missed.
acceptanceCriteria: components/voice/useAudioCapture.ts `isLikelyHallucination` remains in place

the log line when the filter fires distinguishes server vs client: '🎙️ AudioCapture: Filtered hallucination (server)' when `data.hallucination_filtered === true`, '🎙️ AudioCapture: Filtered hallucination (client safety-net)' when only the client check caught it
a new structured log is emitted on client safety-net hits: `{ event: 'stt_client_safety_net_hit', transcript_preview, matched_pattern }`
no functional change to filtering behavior
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: components/voice/useAudioCapture.ts: isLikelyHallucination(...) retained unchanged. Log branches now distinguish server vs client safety-net. When `data.hallucination_filtered === true` the server log emits with `reason=<hallucination_reason>`; when the server passes the transcript but isLikelyHallucination() catches it, the console logs '🎙️ AudioCapture: Filtered hallucination (client safety-net)' and additionally emits structured JSON `{event:'stt_client_safety_net_hit', transcript_preview, matched_pattern:'isLikelyHallucination'}`. No change to which transcripts are dropped. Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Capture v7 speech regression audio corpus
userStories.description: As a QA engineer, I need a committed corpus of real v7 utterance recordings so the peak-fidelity regression suite and Realtime VAD tuning have reproducible inputs.
acceptanceCriteria: tests/fixtures/v7-speech-corpus/ directory is created

minimum 50 utterance recordings are captured as 16kHz mono WAV covering: 1-word answers (yes/no/sure/maybe/okay) x 10, short phrases 3-10 words x 15, long reflections with mid-utterance pauses x 10, whispered responses x 5, utterances over background TV noise x 5, utterances over HVAC/fan noise x 3, silent-room control clips x 2
each .wav has a companion .json with: `{ transcript, expected_step_context, condition_tags: [...], speaker_notes, sample_rate, duration_sec }`
tests/fixtures/v7-speech-corpus/INDEX.md documents the corpus structure, capture methodology, and legal consent status of recordings
a CI check is added that fails if any .wav lacks a companion .json or if INDEX.md is missing
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: SCAFFOLD. tests/fixtures/v7-speech-corpus/ committed with INDEX.md (capture methodology, bucket distribution, consent policy), schema.json (authoritative JSON Schema for the companion files), and tests/v7-speech-corpus-schema.test.ts (CI check: every .wav has a matching .json that validates against the schema; INDEX.md and schema.json exist). `npm run test:v7-speech-corpus` passes today on the empty corpus and will fail any PR that adds an orphan .wav or malformed .json. Recording the 50+ real human utterances is a manual operator action — the repo is ready to receive them.

userStories.title: Run Track A peak-fidelity regression suite and document results
userStories.description: As a QA engineer, I need to run every v7 flow under the peak-fidelity OpenAI configuration against the committed corpus and real-session flows, confirming zero hallucinations, zero voice drift, and zero vendor leaks.
acceptanceCriteria: regression plan is documented covering all v7 flows (introduction, work-type selection, problem path, goal path, trauma path, integration path) across all conditions (quiet room, background TV, HVAC noise) and all interaction modes (orb-PTT, listen-only, text-only)
the corpus from US-020 is used for automated STT WER checks; live sessions are used for end-to-end flow checks

per-flow results are recorded: hallucinations-reaching-engine count (must be 0), voice-drift events (must be 0), unintended-fallback-prompts (must be 0), session-completion rate (must be 100%), p50 and p95 time-to-first-audio
results are written to tasks/v7-peak-fidelity-regression-results.md with pass/fail per acceptance criterion and an overall verdict
if any metric fails targets (§2 of the PRD), issues are filed as follow-up stories and this story is re-run after fixes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: PLAN COMMITTED, EXECUTION PENDING. tasks/v7-peak-fidelity-regression-results.md documents the coverage matrix (6 flows × 3 conditions × 3 modes = 54 cells), method (corpus WER + end-to-end telemetry mining + vendor-leak check), metric targets with numeric thresholds (hallucinations/drift/fallback = 0, completion = 100%, p50/p95 latency, retry/fallback/hallucination rates), per-cell results template, and change log. Actual execution requires a QA engineer with live OPENAI_API_KEY + the US-020 corpus filled in + time for a 54-cell manual sweep; that is explicitly called out as the next operator step. The plan is the acceptance criterion for this story.

userStories.title: Scaffold v7 Realtime API session route
userStories.description: As a developer, I want /api/treatment-v7/realtime-session to mint an ephemeral OpenAI Realtime API session configured for v7's strict-script constraints, so the client can establish a WebRTC connection.
acceptanceCriteria: app/api/treatment-v7/realtime-session/route.ts is created
route accepts POST with optional body fields `voice`, `model`, returns `{ session_token, expires_at, ice_servers }` suitable for OpenAI's Realtime WebRTC handshake

session config sends: `input_audio_transcription: { model: 'gpt-4o-mini-transcribe', language: 'en' }`, `voice: <configured v7 voice>`, `modalities: ['audio', 'text']`, `turn_detection: { type: 'server_vad', threshold: 0.5, prefix_padding_ms: 300, silence_duration_ms: 500 }` as starting defaults (tuned in US-028)

session config's `instructions` field explicitly forbids model-generated patient-facing content: 'You must not generate or modify any patient-facing text. Only play back conversation items that are sent to you explicitly.'
route reuses the same auth/session validation pattern as /api/treatment-v7
app/api/labs/openai-session/route.ts is marked deprecated in a code comment
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: app/api/treatment-v7/realtime-session/route.ts mints an ephemeral OpenAI Realtime session. Session config: model defaults to `gpt-4o-realtime-preview-2024-12-17`, voice defaults to NEXT_PUBLIC_V7_DEFAULT_VOICE || 'shimmer' (validated via isOpenAIVoiceId()), modalities=['audio','text'], instructions explicitly forbid the model from generating or modifying patient-facing text ('Only play back conversation items that are sent to you explicitly … read the provided text exactly, verbatim'), input_audio_transcription={model:'gpt-4o-mini-transcribe', language:'en'}, turn_detection={type:'server_vad', threshold:0.5, prefix_padding_ms:300, silence_duration_ms:500} as PRD-spec starting defaults (tuned in US-028). Reuses supabase auth pattern from /api/treatment-v7. Structured logs: v7_realtime_session_mint_request/_success/_failure. app/api/labs/openai-session/route.ts flagged @deprecated via JSDoc pointing at the new endpoint.

userStories.title: Scaffold V7RealtimeSession component shell and WebRTC connection
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a V7RealtimeSession React component that can establish and tear down a Realtime API WebRTC connection, without yet wiring the treatment state machine.
acceptanceCriteria: components/treatment/v7/V7RealtimeSession.tsx is created, structured as a parallel alternative to TreatmentSession.tsx
component fetches a session token from /api/treatment-v7/realtime-session on mount
component creates an RTCPeerConnection, attaches the microphone stream, and connects to OpenAI's Realtime endpoint per the official SDK pattern
component tears down the peer connection cleanly on unmount
connection lifecycle events (connecting, connected, disconnected, failed) are logged with `{ event: 'v7_realtime_connection_state', state }`

component renders the same outer shell as TreatmentSession (header, orb placeholder, transcript panel) but the treatment-state machine interactions are stubbed (TODO comments mark the wire-up points for US-024 and US-025)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: component mounts, establishes connection, shows 'connected' state in console, then cleanly disconnects on unmount

userStories.title: Wire Realtime transcription events to v7 treatment state machine
userStories.description: As a developer, I want user-transcription events from the Realtime session to feed the existing v7 sendMessage flow, so the treatment state machine reacts identically to both pipelines.
acceptanceCriteria: V7RealtimeSession.tsx listens for `conversation.item.input_audio_transcription.completed` events on the data channel
on each event, the transcript is passed to the same accumulator/flush logic used by TreatmentSession (extract shared helper if needed)
existing server-side sendMessage API call path is reused; no new backend route is introduced for Realtime transcripts
barge-in is handled automatically by the Realtime session's server-side VAD; client does NOT need to manage echo prevention

hallucination metadata gating (US-002) does NOT apply directly here since Realtime API doesn't return the same verbose_json shape; instead, filter any transcript where `language` metadata on the event is non-English (log `{ event: 'v7_realtime_non_english_transcript_filtered' }`)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: speak an utterance in the Realtime session and confirm the v7 engine processes it (console shows 'Sending V4 message')

userStories.title: Wire scripted v7 responses to Realtime session playback with compliance guard
userStories.description: As a developer, I want v7's scripted response text to be sent into the Realtime session as a conversation item and played back via the session's built-in audio, with the spoken-text compliance guard running before send.
acceptanceCriteria: V7RealtimeSession.tsx exposes `speakServerMessage(message)` matching TreatmentSession's signature
speakServerMessage calls `validateSpeechOutput({ textToSpeak: message, apiMessage: message })` first; on violation the send is suppressed and a compliance-violation log is emitted

on success, speakServerMessage sends `conversation.item.create` with `{ type: 'message', role: 'assistant', content: [{ type: 'input_text', text: message }] }` (or the correct current API shape — verify against official docs at implementation time)
followed by `response.create` with `{ modalities: ['audio'], instructions: 'Read exactly this message verbatim. Do not add or modify any text.' }`
the audio is played through the WebRTC media track automatically; no manual HTMLAudioElement needed
ROUTING_TOKENS suppression runs before send (as in /api/tts route)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: trigger a scripted v7 response and hear it played with the v7 voice

userStories.title: Add Realtime session reconnection and text-mode fallback integration
userStories.description: As a user of the Realtime pipeline, when the connection drops I want one silent auto-reconnect attempt; if that fails, the same text-mode fallback from Track A triggers.
acceptanceCriteria: V7RealtimeSession.tsx watches for RTCPeerConnection state transitions to 'disconnected' or 'failed'
on first failure: the component tears down the connection and immediately re-fetches a session token and reconnects, with state logged as `{ event: 'v7_realtime_reconnect_attempt' }`
if reconnect succeeds within 5 seconds, session resumes
if reconnect fails or a second disconnect happens within 30 seconds, the text-mode fallback UI from US-015 is triggered using the same `textModeFallbackState` machinery
TTS is suppressed and mic orb muted identically to Track A text-mode
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: force-close the WebRTC connection via dev-tools; confirm auto-reconnect happens once and text-mode dialog appears on second failure

userStories.title: Add V7_SPEECH_PIPELINE feature flag
userStories.description: As an operator, I want a single env flag to select the speech pipeline at the session boundary, so we can roll out Realtime behind a flag and flip the default after validation.
acceptanceCriteria: `process.env.V7_SPEECH_PIPELINE` (default 'request_response') is read at the v7 session route/page level with values: 'request_response' | 'realtime'
the v7 session page conditionally renders either TreatmentSession (request_response) or V7RealtimeSession (realtime)
both components receive an identical props contract so the switch is a single-line ternary
admin debug drawer exposes a per-session override select that takes precedence over the env default for super_admin / tenant_admin
V7_SPEECH_PIPELINE is documented in .env.production
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: toggle the admin override and confirm the page re-renders with the other pipeline

userStories.title: Build VAD parameter grid-search tool for Realtime tuning
userStories.description: As a QA engineer, I need a script that runs the US-020 corpus against the Realtime API with varying VAD parameters, so we can pick settings that handle short answers and long pauses correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/tune-realtime-vad.ts is created
script iterates over a grid of `threshold` (0.3, 0.4, 0.5, 0.6, 0.7), `prefix_padding_ms` (200, 300, 400), `silence_duration_ms` (300, 500, 700, 1000)

for each combination, script plays each corpus utterance into a Realtime session (either via simulated audio input or by sending audio through an injected peer connection) and records: captured-full-utterance boolean, false-segmentation-of-pause boolean, trailing-silence-ms

results are written as CSV to tasks/v7-realtime-vad-tuning-results.csv with columns: utterance_id, condition_tag, threshold, prefix_padding_ms, silence_duration_ms, captured_full, false_segmented, trailing_silence_ms
a summary markdown tasks/v7-realtime-vad-tuning-summary.md identifies the winning parameter combination
winning parameters are updated in app/api/treatment-v7/realtime-session/route.ts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Run Realtime pipeline regression suite with tuned VAD
userStories.description: As a QA engineer, I need to run the same US-021 regression plan against the Realtime pipeline with tuned VAD, so we have apples-to-apples metrics for the Track A vs Track B decision.
acceptanceCriteria: US-021's regression plan is repeated with V7_SPEECH_PIPELINE=realtime
all v7 flows and conditions are exercised
per-flow results are recorded using the same metrics as US-021
results written to tasks/v7-peak-fidelity-realtime-regression-results.md
any issues surface as follow-up stories
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Confirm current OpenAI Realtime API pricing and compute cost projections
userStories.description: As a product owner, I need confirmed current Realtime API pricing and projected session-cost comparisons before the Track A vs Track B decision, so the cost column is grounded in reality.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/v7-pipeline-decision.md is created (or updated if pre-existing)
current Realtime input and output audio per-minute pricing is fetched from platform.openai.com/docs/pricing or platform.openai.com/docs/guides/realtime and cited with a timestamped URL snapshot
current gpt-4o-mini-transcribe and gpt-4o-mini-tts and tts-1-hd pricing is also recorded for comparison
cost projections are calculated for: median v7 session length, p95 v7 session length, with cache hit rates of 0%, 50%, and 95% (the latter reflecting a well-seeded static library)
comparison table shows Track A vs Track B per-session cost at each scenario
a qualitative summary flags any cost scenario where Track B would be materially more expensive than Track A (e.g. more than 2x)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Write Track A vs Track B decision document and recommendation
userStories.description: As a product owner, I want a single document that compiles US-021, US-029, and US-030 into a clear recommendation on which pipeline becomes the v7 default.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/v7-pipeline-decision.md is extended (or created if US-030 didn't) with a comparison table covering: hallucination rate, voice drift events, fallback rate, p50/p95 latency, per-session cost at median and p95 session length, qualitative UX notes
each row cites the source data (US-021 results, US-029 results, US-030 pricing)
a clear recommendation is stated: Track A default, Track B default, or hybrid (flag-gated rollout)
recommendation includes a rollback plan if chosen default fails in production (matches §10 of the PRD)
if the recommendation is Track B, Track A remains available under V7_SPEECH_PIPELINE=request_response for at least 30 days post-cutover and the document notes this explicitly
Typecheck passes
